2007: Record-low Arctic sea ice extent highlighting rapid polar warming.
Intense European heatwave with widespread temperature anomalies.
Unusual Indian monsoon variability affecting rainfall distribution.
2010: Russian heatwave and wildfires linked to persistent blocking patterns.
Pakistan mega-floods following exceptional monsoon rainfall.
Amazon drought stressing one of the world’s largest carbon sinks.
2015: Strong El Niño event driving global temperature records.
Drought conditions in parts of Africa and Southeast Asia.
Unusually active tropical cyclone season in the Pacific basin.
2016: Hottest year on record at the time, driven by El Niño and long-term warming.
Coral bleaching across the Great Barrier Reef.
Extreme wildfire seasons in several mid-latitude regions.
2019: Record-breaking European heatwaves across multiple countries.
Severe drought and wildfires in the Amazon rainforest.
Typhoon and hurricane activity causing high-impact coastal flooding.
2020: Siberian heatwave with temperatures far above average.
Australian bushfire climate anomaly following prolonged drought.
Record Atlantic hurricane season with numerous named storms.
2023: Exceptional global heat with multiple monthly temperature records.
Marine heatwaves impacting North Atlantic and other ocean basins.
Severe flooding events in several regions linked to extreme rainfall.
